title: The changing of the wilderness; catching fish near Elk River; clear-cutting on Glover Creek
description: The wilderness it used to be, and what it has be come. Catching fish near Elk River as fast as you could pull in the line. Clear-cutting on Glover Creek.

title: Drinking in early Princeton; drunks lie all over on the Fourth of July
description: Drinking in early Princeton: a man gets rolled; drunks lie all over the road on the Fourth of July.

title: Emmett wins at a ball-throwing concession
description: Emmett wins at a ball-throwing concession until he starts to show off.

title: Bootleggers from Montana; flying booze into a field near Potlatch at night
description: Bootleggers from Montana drive armor-plated Studebakers. Flying booze into an improvised landing field near Potlatch at night.

title: Chinese killed on Camas Creek; open prospecting holes in the hills
description: Many Chinese killed on Camas Creek. Open prospecting holes in the hills are dangerous.

title: Poor stranger murdered in the hills
description: A poor stranger, suspected of bank robbery, is murdered in the hills.

title: Sheriff orders Emmett to shoot at any green car that goes by
description: After a bank robbery, the sheriff orders Emmett to shoot at any green car that goes by.

title: Bob broke the law as a young man
description: Bob, who broke the law once as a young man, is hounded ever after.

title: Native Americans in the country; Emmett riding a horse as a boy; huckleberries stolen by Emmett's gang
description: Indians in the country: Emmett, riding on a horse as a boy, is scared by them; they plead with his grandfather to pick camas; they silently ask for the return of a frying pan, left behind the year before; their huckleberries are stolen by one of Emmett's gang.

title: Someone tries to rob Emmett's parents' house at night
description: Gypsy women clean out Guernsey's store, while the men talk up a horse race. Someone tries to rob Emmett's parents' house at night.

title: Rumor that Chinese gold still buried in the hills
description: Rumor that Chinese gold is still buried in the hills. Chinese and Russians have become good guys overnight.

title: Land changing hands; father and partners coming to Palouse
description: Land changed hands a lot in the earliest days. His father and partners got to Palouse by ride-and-tying. Work done by exchange.

title: Trading six horses for down-payment on first car; farmers trying to stop machines
description: Father traded six horses for down-payment on first car. Farmers tried to stop machines by yelling "Whoa". Getting around by horse.

title: Pulling through the Depression; farms were self-sufficient; making petticoats from Bluestem Flour bags
description: Pulling through the Depression "on a cow's tail". Farms were self-sufficient: growing their own food; carding wool; making petticoats from Bluestem Flour bags. If a depression hit now, people would be helpless.

title: IWW's won better working conditions; everything wrong was blamed on IWW's
description: IWW's won the better working conditions for loggers. You never knew who was an IWW. Everything that went wrong was blamed on them. They disappeared when aims were achieved.

title: Ku Klux Klan were vigilantes
description: Ku Klux Klan were vigilantes when Emmett was a boy.

title: Potlatch mill first opened; Four-L's; IWW's sabotaged the company; Emmett almost saws through a sack of dynamite
description: IWW's fought for better conditions for farmworker too. Potlatch mill first opened with two twelve hour shifts a day. Four-L's, the "Long, Lazy, Lousy Lumbermen," intended to stop the men from organizing. IWW's did just enough sabotage to drive the company nuts. Emmett almost saws through a sack of dynamite.

title: Propaganda against IWW; acts of sabotage on farms; improving farm labor conditions
description: Propaganda against IWW, as the enemy. Acts of sabotage on farms, and their effect in improving farm labor conditions.
